Pros
  • Steady high-end performance with no throttling
  • Per-key RGB lighting makes the keyboard pop
  • Chassis redesign looks good and offers better airflow
  • High-end 240Hz QHD+ display makes games look great
Cons
  • No more SD card reader
  • Screen has a 16:9 aspect ratio, could be brighter
  • Chassis is a fingerprint magnet
  • More pronounced performance drop on DC power
$1150 at HP (Intel H-series) $1600 at HP (Intel HX-series) $1759 at Amazon

HP Omen 16 (2023): Pricing and availability

HP offers two tiers of customizable Omen 16t-wf000 models, as well as a number of pre-configured setups. The ones with Intel's 13th Gen H-series processors (CPU) and Nvidia RTX 3050 and 4050 laptop-class graphics cards (GPU) are more affordable. Displays max out at a 1920x1080 (FHD) resolution at 165Hz, and there's no per-key RGB lighting option. The least you'll pay is $1,150 (on sale for $850 at the time of writing).

The second tier of customizable models features Intel's more powerful 13th Gen HX-series chips and Nvidia RTX 4060, 4070, and 4080 Laptop GPUs. A display with 2560x1440 (QHD) resolution and per-key RGB keyboard lighting also becomes available. This tier starts at $1,600 for a model with Core i7-13700HX CPU, RTX 4060 Laptop GPU, 16GB of RAM, 512GB solid-state drive (SSD), and FHD display at 165Hz.

HP's Omen laptops often see deep discounts; wait for a sale to maximize the value.

The model I have for review includes a 13th Gen Intel Core i7-13700HX CPU, Nvidia RTX 4080 Laptop GPU with a 145W TGP rating, 32GB of dual-channel DDR5-4800MHz RAM, and a 2TB M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD. The keyboard has per-key RGB lighting, and the display has a 2560x1400 (QHD) resolution, 240Hz refresh rate, and 3ms response time. It currently costs about $2,970 as configured at HP.

When you order the RTX 4080 GPU, HP will also add a HyperX Cloud II Core Wireless gaming headset worth about $120. HP purchased HyperX in 2021 for $425 million and has since been mixing its suite of products in with the Omen and Victus brands. One last thing to mention is HP's Omen Transcend laptop, which I reviewed a few weeks after the standard Omen 16. These are the most premium gaming laptops that HP offers, with a mini-LED QHD display option and an IR camera for Windows Hello. These usually start at $1,700.

You can also find the HP Omen 16 at other retailers like Amazon and Best Buy, but you won't find as many configurations available. HP's Omen laptops often see deep discounts; wait for a sale to maximize the value.

Design and ports

New trunk design makes ports more accessible

HP made some big changes to the Omen 16's chassis for 2023. The base portion of the laptop is about a third of an inch deeper than the 2022 model, and the lid's hinges are now set forward on the body. This leaves what HP calls a "trunk" at the back of the laptop, a look that has been popularized in recent years by other brands like Lenovo and MSI. Wide exhaust vents take up most of the space, with the ports — HDMI 2.1, RJ45 Ethernet at 2.5Gbps, USB-A 3.0 (5Gbps), and a barrel charging port — in the middle. This keeps your more permanent connections out of the way when you're plugged in at a desk.

The sides have visibly larger exhaust vents to match the back. On the left are two Thunderbolt 4 and a 3.5mm audio jack; on the right is another USB-A port. HP removed the SD card reader from the Omen 16 (2023), a move against those using a PC for mixed-use. A decent Thunderbolt dock is the next best thing to re-add these productivity ports.

Opt for the RTX 4080 configuration and HP will throw in a HyperX Cloud II Core Wireless headset with automatic laptop pairing.

Elsewhere in connectivity, it leans on Bluetooth 5.3 and either an Intel or Realtek Wi-Fi 6E module for wireless connectivity (based on your config's other hardware). The M.2 Wi-Fi card is upgradeable after purchase, should anything go wrong.

HP does seem to have made headset pairing as easy as possible, but only if you're using a bundled HyperX headset (not available with all Omen models). A small module inside the laptop pairs the headset automatically whenever you power the PC, which saves you a port. I wasn't able to test the signal quality myself, but it's a neat way to get HyperX out there.

The all-black matte finish that HP uses on the chassis is nothing out of the ordinary, but that's not a drawback. It lets the keyboard RGB pop particularly well, and it makes the laptop look thinner even when sitting closed. An aggressive wedge shape on the bottom gives way to an enormous intake vent that's propped well off any flat surface. The laptop's lid, no longer adorned by the diamond Omen logo, has a trim badge with the brand's lettering instead. The laptop looks more professional because of that change alone. Just make sure you tuck a cleaning cloth into your laptop bag since the finish has a tendency to pick up smudges.

Build quality is just above average. There's no creaking when the laptop is handled and twisted, but the bottom panel feels loose in the center. The lid cover isn't particularly rigid either, and it seems like hard plastic was mainly used aside from the alloy keyboard surround. Everything comes together seamlessly, however, and the PC is smooth to handle.

The Omen 16's new port trunk makes cable management so much easier.

Sound comes booming from the dual speakers installed toward the front of the chassis on the wedge base. They're angled so that your desk doesn't muffle them, and they're covered by a large pinhole grille. There's another grille located between the keyboard and display, but it seems to be a thermal feature instead of hiding any more audio hardware.

Embedded into the laptop's top bezel is a webcam with a physical shutter. The move from 720p to 1080p is one of the big changes to the Omen 16, giving would-be streamers and collaborators a built-in tool more fitting for 2023. Video enhancements tied in with Nvidia Studio are available within the included Omen app. The camera's extra pixels provide more clarity, but I would still be looking at a better webcam if I was frequently streaming. The lack of an IR sensor for Windows Hello will be an annoyance for some.

Keyboard

The RGB shines

The keyboard hasn't seen any changes since the previous generation. I'm still not a diehard fan of the flat, chiclet-style keycaps that HP uses, but I love the font and the overall layout. Navigation keys are clustered together off to the side for easy access, with shortcuts straight to the Omen or Calculator apps also included. My unit has per-key RGB lighting that shines through the key's lettering for a more premium look. This is all customizable through the Omen app.

I typed thousands of words on this keyboard without any issues. The oversized spacebar, deep travel, and firm actuation are all appreciated. Last year's Omen 16 had a larger touchpad that suffered from a hollow click, but that seems to have been remedied here. The touchpad is just a tad smaller, but it tracks without fault and has a firm click. The laptop is set up primarily for gaming, but the keyboard and touchpad are together also a win for anyone using the laptop for work.

Display

QHD+ at 240Hz with a 16:9 aspect ratio

My Omen 16 review unit has the upgraded IPS display option with 2560x1440 (QHD) resolution, 240Hz variable refresh rate, 3ms response time, and anti-glare finish. You have to step up to the more premium Omen Transcend 16 if you'd like to land a powerful laptop with a mini-LED display.

I tested the Omen 16's screen with my SpyderX Pro colorimeter, revealing 99% sRGB, 78% AdobeRGB, and 79% AdobeRGB color gamut coverage. These are specs in line with most mid-range gaming laptops. Your favorite titles are going to look full and colorful, and you'll have no issue with standard productivity work. However, the display will fall behind if you need to use it for a specialized task that needs better coverage in non-sRGB gamuts.

The high refresh rate and low blue light certification should, in theory, reduce eye strain, and the anti-glare finish does a great job of helping the maximum 340 nits (as tested) look brighter in well-lit or sunny rooms. A bit more natural brightness would go a long way, especially since this is considered the top display option.

The Omen 16's QHD display with 240Hz refresh rate is a perfect pairing with the laptop's fastest hardware options.

My biggest gripe with the display is the 16:9 aspect ratio. At a time when most major manufacturers have moved on to 16:10, HP is holding strong with the narrower look. Not only does this leave a large chin below the screen, but it also reduces the amount of screen real estate you have to work with. The ratio is less noticeable when gaming or watching videos; moreso when browsing the web or handling email.

In my HP Omen Transcend 16 review, I noted that it's the only version of the Omen laptops with a taller 16:10 aspect ratio. Its QHD+ resolution option is also your entry to a great mini-LED panel with outstanding color, contrast, and brightness. The drawback? It costs considerably more than the standard Omen 16.

Performance, battery, and software

Better cooling for the upgraded CPU and GPU

HP sent us a high-end configuration to test, with a 13th-generation Intel Core i7-13700HX CPU, RTX 4080 Laptop GPU, 32GB RAM, and 2TB SSD. This is about as high as the laptop's specs climb, though you can upgrade RAM and storage after purchase thanks to an accessible internal layout. I put this hardware through a stress test for 15 minutes to see how it handles heat.

While the hardware is all tuned on the go with AI to prevent throttling, the laptop still needs ample cooling to allow the CPU and GPU to run as freely as possible. The Omen 16's new cooling system — with improved exhaust venting and redesigned fans — seems to be up to the task. I ran a stress test for 15 minutes to see how the system handles a full load. The GPU hit 143W at peak (its maximum TGP is 145W) but evened out at 124W for the majority of the run. The CPU sat at about 45W, with core temperatures hovering between 68 and 75 degrees Celsius. There was absolutely no throttling during the stress test.

The Omen 16's new cooling system — with improved exhaust venting and redesigned fans — seems to be up to the task.

Fans topped out at 58.5dB during the stress test, in line or slightly above the fan noise from other high-end gaming laptops. This is a lot of power inside a slim chassis, but HP seems to have done a proper job on the cooling redesign. The exterior of the laptop never got too hot to handle, and most of the heat is gathered toward the center of the chassis.

I ran a bunch of synthetic benchmarks to see how this Omen 16 compares to the 2022 model, as well as the other HP gaming laptops refreshed in 2023.

Benchmark

HP Omen 16 (2023), Intel Core i7-13700HX, RTX 4080

HP Omen 16 (2022), Intel Core i7-12700H, RTX 3070 Ti

HP Omen Transcend (2023), Intel Core i9-13900HX, RTX 4070

HP Victus 16 (2023), Intel Core i7-13800HX, RTX 4050

PCMark 10

7,523

7,119

7,537

7,755

Cinebench R23 (single / multi)

1,852 / 19,638

1,845 / 14,464

16,740

3DMark Time Spy Extreme

7,589

5,537

5,866

4,221

Not listed in the table above are the SSD's performance numbers. An SK hynix drive is on board, hitting 7,138MB/s read and 6,414MB/s write speeds. It's good to see that HP isn't skimping on storage speed since it's a crucial aspect of any gaming laptop.

As with most gaming laptops running this level of hardware, you're going to need an AC adapter nearby when you want to have some fun. The 83Wh battery lasted about an hour and 19 minutes in PCMark 10's Gaming rundown test with the system set to Performance mode. I also tested the laptop with a reduced 60Hz screen refresh rate and Balanced power modes. In PCMark 10's Modern Office rundown, the Omen 16 lasted three hours and 33 minutes under these conditions. I saw slightly better everyday battery life from the recent run of Legion laptops, but overall it's not out of the ordinary.

The HP Omen 16's performance drops off significantly when running on battery power.

Performance on DC power is below what I expected. PCMark 10's standard test for full-system performance fell to 5,942 on battery power from the 7,523 AC power score. Carrasqueira called out this same occurrence in his Omen 16 (2022) review. Bottom line? Stick with AC power when going about intensive tasks; HP has tuned the laptop to be a beast when plugged in.

Here's a look at how the Omen 16 (2023) fares in standard gaming situations. Note that the Legion Pro 5 has an RTX 4070, while the Legion Pro 5i has an RTX 4060. The RTX 4080 does a lot of heavy lifting in these benchmarks, but the Core i7-13700HX is no slouch.

Game

Graphics setting

HP Omen 16 (2023) Average frame rate (QHD)

HP Omen Transcend (2023) Average frame rate (QHD+)

HP Victus 16 (2023) Average frame rate (FHD)

Red Dead Redemption 2

Ultra / No DLSS

103 FPS

101 FPS

122 FPS

The screen's 240Hz refresh rate isn't really pushed with these titles, but you should be able to get close with less intensive games. All games look phenomenal on the system, and I had no problems running through my catalog. Note that this comparison shows the Omen Transcend 16 with RTX 4070 at a QHD+ resolution, as well as the Victus 16 with RTX 4050 and an FHD resolution. That explains some of the disparity between results.

Part of the Omen app is reserved for automatic game tuning powered by Nvidia. You can also set the system up to boost performance whenever it senses a game, closing thirsty background processes to maximize power for fun. The app has worked well so far, though I wish it was a bit easier to get into the system performance options when it loads.

Should you buy the HP Omen 16 (2023)?

You should buy the HP Omen 16 (2023) if:

  • You need a laptop that's mainly used for gaming
  • You're looking for an excellent value
  • You want a system that can handle all modern titles at smooth framerates

You should not buy the HP Omen 16 (2023) if:

  • You're in need of a laptop that can handle other tasks (like creative work) equally well
  • You want the best battery life possible from a gaming laptop
  • You want a 4K display

HP's Omen 16 was refreshed for 2023 with more of a focus on delivering a quality PC gaming experience while also being able to keep up with the other demands of modern users. The 1080p camera and tuned mics are better suited for game streaming and video collaboration; the keyboard is set up to accommodate those who type all day; and the screen's anti-glare finish can deal with office lighting. Just don't try using it outdoors. I really do love the look of this laptop, and I'm glad that HP redesigned the chassis for better airflow. It looks more modern, and the trunk allows for your key ports to remain out of the way during use at a desk.

But HP also took some steps backward. The SD card reader was removed, battery life has suffered, the screen is still using a 16:9 aspect ratio, and its chassis is physically larger.

Some won't be able to live with these shortcomings, but the Omen 16 offers excellent value at around $1,150 for the introductory model. Performance is unthrottled thanks to automatic AI tuning, the laptop keeps its cool with redesigned venting and fans, and the 240Hz QHD screen can easily keep up with the killer GPU power.

The Omen 16 (2023) is the best gaming-focused laptop from HP's stable.

If we look at a similar model from the Omen Transcend series, you're on the hook for about $1,700 just to start. And if you head the other way, toward the HP Victus series, models start at about $950. In my HP Victus 16 (2023) review, I remarked that it "would be easy to recommend if it wasn't so close to the Omen 16 in price." That still holds true today.

Out of the three laptops, I'd reserve the Omen 16 reviewed here for a focused gaming experience. It doesn't cost that much more than the Victus, and it brings an altogether higher-end experience when it comes to build quality and features. The Omen 16 also doesn't cost nearly as much as the Transcend, and it comes with the RTX 4080 Laptop GPU as a configurable option.

Whether the Omen 16 is for you — like its 2022 predecessor — really depends on your priorities. HP made advancements this year in terms of ease of mixed-use, but it's still plain to see that it's set up first for gaming. However, HP offers frequent laptop discounts that can save you hundreds of dollars. So maybe that value is worth it.

HP Omen 16 (2023)
An excellent value
8 / 10

The HP Omen 16 (2023) brings improved performance and cooling, along with some new key features like a 1080p webcam and redesigned port layout. It has some shortcomings, but it's still a great value for PC gamers.

$1150 at HP (Intel H-series) $1600 at HP (Intel HX-series) $1759 at Amazon

The Omen 16's new port trunk makes cable management so much easier.

Sound comes booming from the dual speakers installed toward the front of the chassis on the wedge base. They're angled so that your desk doesn't muffle them, and they're covered by a large pinhole grille. There's another grille located between the keyboard and display, but it seems to be a thermal feature instead of hiding any more audio hardware.

Embedded into the laptop's top bezel is a webcam with a physical shutter. The move from 720p to 1080p is one of the big changes to the Omen 16, giving would-be streamers and collaborators a built-in tool more fitting for 2023. Video enhancements tied in with Nvidia Studio are available within the included Omen app. The camera's extra pixels provide more clarity, but I would still be looking at a better webcam if I was frequently streaming. The lack of an IR sensor for Windows Hello will be an annoyance for some.

Keyboard

The RGB shines

The keyboard hasn't seen any changes since the previous generation. I'm still not a diehard fan of the flat, chiclet-style keycaps that HP uses, but I love the font and the overall layout. Navigation keys are clustered together off to the side for easy access, with shortcuts straight to the Omen or Calculator apps also included. My unit has per-key RGB lighting that shines through the key's lettering for a more premium look. This is all customizable through the Omen app.

I typed thousands of words on this keyboard without any issues. The oversized spacebar, deep travel, and firm actuation are all appreciated. Last year's Omen 16 had a larger touchpad that suffered from a hollow click, but that seems to have been remedied here. The touchpad is just a tad smaller, but it tracks without fault and has a firm click. The laptop is set up primarily for gaming, but the keyboard and touchpad are together also a win for anyone using the laptop for work.

Display

QHD+ at 240Hz with a 16:9 aspect ratio

My Omen 16 review unit has the upgraded IPS display option with 2560x1440 (QHD) resolution, 240Hz variable refresh rate, 3ms response time, and anti-glare finish. You have to step up to the more premium Omen Transcend 16 if you'd like to land a powerful laptop with a mini-LED display.

I tested the Omen 16's screen with my SpyderX Pro colorimeter, revealing 99% sRGB, 78% AdobeRGB, and 79% AdobeRGB color gamut coverage. These are specs in line with most mid-range gaming laptops. Your favorite titles are going to look full and colorful, and you'll have no issue with standard productivity work. However, the display will fall behind if you need to use it for a specialized task that needs better coverage in non-sRGB gamuts.

The high refresh rate and low blue light certification should, in theory, reduce eye strain, and the anti-glare finish does a great job of helping the maximum 340 nits (as tested) look brighter in well-lit or sunny rooms. A bit more natural brightness would go a long way, especially since this is considered the top display option.

The Omen 16's QHD display with 240Hz refresh rate is a perfect pairing with the laptop's fastest hardware options.

My biggest gripe with the display is the 16:9 aspect ratio. At a time when most major manufacturers have moved on to 16:10, HP is holding strong with the narrower look. Not only does this leave a large chin below the screen, but it also reduces the amount of screen real estate you have to work with. The ratio is less noticeable when gaming or watching videos; moreso when browsing the web or handling email.

In my HP Omen Transcend 16 review, I noted that it's the only version of the Omen laptops with a taller 16:10 aspect ratio. Its QHD+ resolution option is also your entry to a great mini-LED panel with outstanding color, contrast, and brightness. The drawback? It costs considerably more than the standard Omen 16.

Performance, battery, and software

Better cooling for the upgraded CPU and GPU

HP sent us a high-end configuration to test, with a 13th-generation Intel Core i7-13700HX CPU, RTX 4080 Laptop GPU, 32GB RAM, and 2TB SSD. This is about as high as the laptop's specs climb, though you can upgrade RAM and storage after purchase thanks to an accessible internal layout. I put this hardware through a stress test for 15 minutes to see how it handles heat.

While the hardware is all tuned on the go with AI to prevent throttling, the laptop still needs ample cooling to allow the CPU and GPU to run as freely as possible. The Omen 16's new cooling system — with improved exhaust venting and redesigned fans — seems to be up to the task. I ran a stress test for 15 minutes to see how the system handles a full load. The GPU hit 143W at peak (its maximum TGP is 145W) but evened out at 124W for the majority of the run. The CPU sat at about 45W, with core temperatures hovering between 68 and 75 degrees Celsius. There was absolutely no throttling during the stress test.

The Omen 16's new cooling system — with improved exhaust venting and redesigned fans — seems to be up to the task.

Fans topped out at 58.5dB during the stress test, in line or slightly above the fan noise from other high-end gaming laptops. This is a lot of power inside a slim chassis, but HP seems to have done a proper job on the cooling redesign. The exterior of the laptop never got too hot to handle, and most of the heat is gathered toward the center of the chassis.

I ran a bunch of synthetic benchmarks to see how this Omen 16 compares to the 2022 model, as well as the other HP gaming laptops refreshed in 2023.

Benchmark

HP Omen 16 (2023), Intel Core i7-13700HX, RTX 4080

HP Omen 16 (2022), Intel Core i7-12700H, RTX 3070 Ti

HP Omen Transcend (2023), Intel Core i9-13900HX, RTX 4070

HP Victus 16 (2023), Intel Core i7-13800HX, RTX 4050

PCMark 10

3DMark Time Spy Extreme

7,589

5,537

5,866

4,221

Not listed in the table above are the SSD's performance numbers. An SK hynix drive is on board, hitting 7,138MB/s read and 6,414MB/s write speeds. It's good to see that HP isn't skimping on storage speed since it's a crucial aspect of any gaming laptop.

As with most gaming laptops running this level of hardware, you're going to need an AC adapter nearby when you want to have some fun. The 83Wh battery lasted about an hour and 19 minutes in PCMark 10's Gaming rundown test with the system set to Performance mode. I also tested the laptop with a reduced 60Hz screen refresh rate and Balanced power modes. In PCMark 10's Modern Office rundown, the Omen 16 lasted three hours and 33 minutes under these conditions. I saw slightly better everyday battery life from the recent run of Legion laptops, but overall it's not out of the ordinary.

The HP Omen 16's performance drops off significantly when running on battery power.

Performance on DC power is below what I expected. PCMark 10's standard test for full-system performance fell to 5,942 on battery power from the 7,523 AC power score. Carrasqueira called out this same occurrence in his Omen 16 (2022) review. Bottom line? Stick with AC power when going about intensive tasks; HP has tuned the laptop to be a beast when plugged in.

Here's a look at how the Omen 16 (2023) fares in standard gaming situations. Note that the Legion Pro 5 has an RTX 4070, while the Legion Pro 5i has an RTX 4060. The RTX 4080 does a lot of heavy lifting in these benchmarks, but the Core i7-13700HX is no slouch.

Game

Graphics setting

HP Omen 16 (2023) Average frame rate (QHD)

HP Omen Transcend (2023) Average frame rate (QHD+)

HP Victus 16 (2023) Average frame rate (FHD)

103 FPS

The screen's 240Hz refresh rate isn't really pushed with these titles, but you should be able to get close with less intensive games. All games look phenomenal on the system, and I had no problems running through my catalog. Note that this comparison shows the Omen Transcend 16 with RTX 4070 at a QHD+ resolution, as well as the Victus 16 with RTX 4050 and an FHD resolution. That explains some of the disparity between results.

Part of the Omen app is reserved for automatic game tuning powered by Nvidia. You can also set the system up to boost performance whenever it senses a game, closing thirsty background processes to maximize power for fun. The app has worked well so far, though I wish it was a bit easier to get into the system performance options when it loads.

Should you buy the HP Omen 16 (2023)?

You should buy the HP Omen 16 (2023) if:

  • You need a laptop that's mainly used for gaming
  • You're looking for an excellent value
  • You want a system that can handle all modern titles at smooth framerates

You should not buy the HP Omen 16 (2023) if:

  • You're in need of a laptop that can handle other tasks (like creative work) equally well
  • You want the best battery life possible from a gaming laptop
  • You want a 4K display

HP's Omen 16 was refreshed for 2023 with more of a focus on delivering a quality PC gaming experience while also being able to keep up with the other demands of modern users. The 1080p camera and tuned mics are better suited for game streaming and video collaboration; the keyboard is set up to accommodate those who type all day; and the screen's anti-glare finish can deal with office lighting. Just don't try using it outdoors. I really do love the look of this laptop, and I'm glad that HP redesigned the chassis for better airflow. It looks more modern, and the trunk allows for your key ports to remain out of the way during use at a desk.

But HP also took some steps backward. The SD card reader was removed, battery life has suffered, the screen is still using a 16:9 aspect ratio, and its chassis is physically larger.

Some won't be able to live with these shortcomings, but the Omen 16 offers excellent value at around $1,150 for the introductory model. Performance is unthrottled thanks to automatic AI tuning, the laptop keeps its cool with redesigned venting and fans, and the 240Hz QHD screen can easily keep up with the killer GPU power.

The Omen 16 (2023) is the best gaming-focused laptop from HP's stable.

If we look at a similar model from the Omen Transcend series, you're on the hook for about $1,700 just to start. And if you head the other way, toward the HP Victus series, models start at about $950. In my HP Victus 16 (2023) review, I remarked that it "would be easy to recommend if it wasn't so close to the Omen 16 in price." That still holds true today.

Out of the three laptops, I'd reserve the Omen 16 reviewed here for a focused gaming experience. It doesn't cost that much more than the Victus, and it brings an altogether higher-end experience when it comes to build quality and features. The Omen 16 also doesn't cost nearly as much as the Transcend, and it comes with the RTX 4080 Laptop GPU as a configurable option.

Whether the Omen 16 is for you — like its 2022 predecessor — really depends on your priorities. HP made advancements this year in terms of ease of mixed-use, but it's still plain to see that it's set up first for gaming. However, HP offers frequent laptop discounts that can save you hundreds of dollars. So maybe that value is worth it.

HP Omen 16 (2023)
An excellent value
8 / 10

The HP Omen 16 (2023) brings improved performance and cooling, along with some new key features like a 1080p webcam and redesigned port layout. It has some shortcomings, but it's still a great value for PC gamers.

$1150 at HP (Intel H-series) $1600 at HP (Intel HX-series) $1759 at Amazon

item.title: Best smartwatches for the iPhone in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best smartwatches for the iPhone in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Smartwatches aren't just gadgets; they're a step towards a more efficient and well-balanced lifestyle. Offering an ingenious blend of technology and fashion, they have become a game-changer in enhancing our daily lives. From fitness tracking and heart rate monitoring to instant notifications and music control, you have everything on your wrist.

Nonetheless, so many options might be confusing, especially if you are an Apple user concerned about getting the right smartwatch for an iPhone. While Apple watches are a straightforward answer, there are other third-party smartwatches as well, offering you better durability and functionality.

  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Series 9
    Editor's choice
    $390 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Ultra 2
    Premium pick
    $774 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Midnight Apple Watch SE 2
    Best value
    $309 at Walmart
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Venu 3
    Best for runners
    $450 at Amazon
  • Source: Fitbit
    Fitbit Charge 5
    Best fitness tracker
    $144 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit Balance
    AI powered fitness coach
    $200 at Amazon
  • Source: Xiaomi
    Xiaomi Band 8
    Cheap fitness tracker
    $53 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit Bip 5
    Extra large screen
    $75 at Amazon

Our top picks for best smartwatches for the iPhone

Apple Watch Series 9
Editor's choice

The ultimate watch for an outstanding performance and value

$390 $399 Save $9

The Apple Watch Series 9 stands out with its blazing S9 chip, boasting a 2x brighter display at 2000 nits. With advanced features like ECG checks and all-day battery life, it's the perfect blend of performance and practicality for iPhone users seeking a mid-range smartwatch.

Pros
  • New Apple S9 Chip
  • Brighter screen at 2,000 nits
  • Wide variety of case colors
Cons
  • Battery life could be improved further
$390 at Amazon

Apple Watch Series 9 is the latest addition to the list of Apple Watches, released in September 2023. What’s new? Well, for starters, it has a 2x brighter display than the Series 8, with a maximum brightness of up to 2000 nits, making it easier to read in full sun and better in low-light situations. The display size is 1.69 inches, which is neither too big nor too small. Plus, It also features a new Double Tap gesture, which allows users to launch apps and control the watch with a simple tap of their thumb and forefinger.

Compared to the Apple Watch Series 8, the Series 9 has a faster S9 chip. The S9 chip is the most powerful watch chip yet, with a dual-core CPU that has 5.6 billion transistors and a four-core Neural Engine that can process machine learning tasks up to twice as fast as the S8 chip. The Series 9 also has expanded Siri processing and the option of a fun pink case and band.

The Series 9 has a 64GB RAM capacity. Screen size variations include 45mm and 41mm, but with a marginally thin price difference, my recommendation would be the 45mm version. Best part? You get to choose your case type. Apple is offering users to select from stainless steel or aluminum cases. But, obviously, stainless steel is much more expensive, as compared to aluminum.

With several advanced features, such as ECG check, time spent in deep sleep, blood oxygen level monitoring, and several others, it is by-far one of the best smartwatches for iPhone in 2023.

With up to 18 hours of battery life (for GPS+Cellular variant), you’ll be able to use it for the entire day easily. In a nutshell, it really is the ideal choice for someone who cannot put in a big chunk of money for Apple Watch Ultra or Ultra 2, but still wants a decent Apple smartwatch.

Apple Watch Ultra 2
Premium pick

An excellent choice for managing fitness with luxury

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 shines with a dazzling 3000-nit display and a robust titanium case, offering both style and durability. It's the top-tier choice for those seeking a brilliant smartwatch experience.

Pros
  • Maximum brightness of 3000 nits
  • Titanium case gives it maximum protection
  • Double tap gesture
Cons
  • Priciest smartwatch for iPhone
$774 at Amazon

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 is a premium smartwatch offering from Apple, featuring a range of advanced capabilities. The Ultra 2 has a brighter display, with a maximum brightness of up to 3000 nits, compared to the original Ultra's 2000-nit display. It also features a 49mm corrosion-resistant titanium case which gives it points for durability. Also, multiple colors and sizes enable you precise customization.

GPS and Cellular connectivity are both provided, and it offers 40m water-resistance too, which makes it ideal for water sports enthusiasts. As for health management, this watch is packed with loads of metrics including ECG, blood sugar, and heart health monitor.

When compared to the previous Apple Watch Ultra, the Ultra 2 offers several improvements, including the new S9 SiP with a 64-bit dual-core processor, a customizable Action button, and Double Tap gesture as well. Another new thing we notice is Precision Finding for iPhone 15 models, which is not available in the original Ultra.

As far as the battery is concerned, Apple claims a 36-hour battery life on regular use, and 72-hour battery life in low-power mode. Realistically speaking, I would say it would be more like 30 hours and 60 hours, respectively. Nonetheless, it really depends on your usage. But one thing is for sure, the battery timing is ample to keep you running the entire day even with ample use, and even good enough when traveling long distances.

One thing to really consider is the high weight of 61.4g, which may be of inconvenience to a few users. Specifically, I would recommend this watch to someone looking for a really bright display, since no other Apple Watch comes at a peak brightness of 3000 nits. Plus, the battery timing is really great. The other features are also available in Apple Watch Series 9, such as the S9 Chip.

Midnight Apple Watch SE 2
Best value

A low-priced watch for Apple fans

The Apple Watch SE 2 offers GPS + Cellular models and sleek aluminum finishes. It comes loaded with features including heart rate monitoring, water resistance, and fall detection. The Retina OLED display, powerful chip, and sustainable design make it a versatile and eco-conscious wearable for someone wanting an Apple product on a budget.

Pros
  • Faster charging support (0-80% in 45 minutes)
  • Water-resistant up to 50m
  • 4 times cheaper than Apple Watch Ultra
Cons
  • Does not have Always-on Display
$309 at Walmart

There’s always this sense of trust when it comes to official products, and for that reason, the top 2 of our picks are official Apple products. While it may not be the best, it surely is one of the finest budget picks since it offers 70% of the features offered on Apple Watch Ultra, and is quite identical to Apple Watch Series 8.

The second-generation Apple Watch SE maintains the 44mm and 40mm case options and retains the display size of its predecessors. Offering a resolution of 368x448 pixels, you won’t feel like making a compromise on the display. The Retina LTPO OLED display itself is indeed a cool aspect for many users.

However, it offers half the brightness of the Apple Watch Ultra at 1000 nits. It isn’t really bad unless you are really looking forward to using your watch in extreme lighting conditions.

The real improvement lies within the processor as Apple Watch SE 2 comes with S8 SiP with a 64-bit dual-core processor. This is followed by the W3 Apple Wireless Chip as well. A few tests have shown a 20% improvement in the performance and speed of the Apple Watch SE 2 compared to its predecessors.

The 36-hour battery life is a little less compared to other watches. However, considering all other aspects, including fitness tracking and emergency crash detection, the Apple Watch SE 2 is a reasonable bargain for someone wanting an Apple product on a budget.

Garmin Venu 3
Best for runners

Sophistication redefined with an impressive battery timing

At half the price of Apple Ultra, you can stay ahead with Garmin's cutting-edge wearable technology. With an extensive focus on health aspects and an amazing battery backup of 11 days, it becomes a dynamic companion for your active lifestyle.

Pros
  • 11-day mega battery backup
  • Provides a fuller picture of one's health
  • 1 hour fast charging support
Cons
  • No map support
$450 at Amazon

The Garmin Venu 3 is the latest addition to Garmin's Venu series of GPS smartwatches and a pretty smart choice for runners. Why? Mainly because the Venu 3 introduces new health and fitness features, including Sleep Coaching, Recovery Time, and Wheelchair Mode, which are not available on the Venu 2.

The Venu 3 comes with a built-in speaker and microphone, allowing users to answer calls and interact with voice assistants directly from the watch. The Venu 3 has a diameter of 45.4 mm and a thickness of 12.2 mm. On top of that, the Venu 3 features a high display resolution of 454 x 454 pixels, providing sharper and more detailed images and text compared to the Venu 2, which had 416 x 416 pixels.

You have the option to choose from 45mm and 41mm. The Venu 3 features a vibrant and sharp 1.4-inch AMOLED touchscreen display which provides a brilliant interface for various activities, from checking notifications to tracking workouts. With up to 14 days of battery life in smartwatch mode, the Venu 3 offers impressive longevity, reducing the need for frequent recharging. However, real-time results will vary.

In addition to that, Venu 3 provides detailed health and wellness insights, including features like Body Battery and advanced sleep monitoring. Body Battery informs users about their energy levels, helping them determine the optimal time for training, while advanced sleep monitoring offers valuable data to improve sleep quality and overall well-being.

Fitbit Charge 5
Best fitness tracker

A lightweight and light-featured watch recommended for beginners

$144 $150 Save $6

For a low price tag, this is definitely one of the best fitness trackers for someone not much into extra features. The Fitbit Charge 5 may not have all the features you’ve heard about, but it does fulfill the basic job: Health and Fitness tracking.

Pros
  • Does not feel too heavy
  • ECG Application Supported
  • 7 days battery life
Cons
  • No physical button on the back
  • Smaller screen than other known watches
  • Does not support music playback
$144 at Amazon

The Fitbit Charge 5 is one of the best fitness trackers on the market and is an ideal choice for someone looking to spend minimally on a smartwatch. However, that doesn’t mean you are compromising on any major element. A 7-day battery backup followed by several fitness tracking parameters makes it quite simple for any user to stay engaged in their wellness journey.

The fact that this watch has ECG supported is kind of unbelievable, noticing the fairly lower price point. It also tracks the trends in your wellness and health aspects by monitoring heart rate variation, breathing rate, pulse rate, and numerous other indicators.

You’ll also notice extra applications offered on this watch to enhance the user experience. Smart notifications, Find My Phone, Silent Alarms, and Smart Wakeup are just a few to name.

However, Charge 5 makes its way down the list because users aren't really impressed by the 14.7mm x 21.9mm screen size. Besides, the user interface itself isn’t neat. Only one of nine metrics is visible at one time, and to access the next metric, a tap is required. Imagine: While you are running, the small screen size makes the text illegible. It just eliminates the point of convenience.

Another downside to this watch is it does not have support for Spotify or music streaming, unlike other popular smartwatches in the market. Overall, it isn't really bad as a smartwatch, given the low price point. It does fulfill the basic purpose of health and fitness tracking.

Amazfit Balance
AI powered fitness coach

Built for the rough and tough ones

$200 $230 Save $30

The Amazfit Balance Smartwatch takes fitness to the next level with its AI-powered fitness coach and an exceptional 1.5-inch AMOLED display, all packed into a lightweight design for ultimate convenience.

Pros
  • Lots of helpful AI features
  • 1.5-inch stunning AMOLED display
  • Lightweight and convenient
Cons
  • No blood pressure monitoring
$200 at Amazon

It's 2023, and we are already seeing a massive rise of AI in modern gadgets and applications, even in smartwatches. The Amazfit Balance Smartwatch is a cutting-edge fitness and health tracker that comes with an AI Fitness Coach. It leverages large language models (LLMs) to provide personalized guidance and support to users. The AI Fitness Coach utilizes user data to generate detailed reports and can offer clear and straightforward answers to user queries.

In addition to the AI Fitness Coach, the Amazfit Balance Smart Watch also features an AI-powered chatbot known as the Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach. This chatbot is designed to provide guidance on sleep and wellness, offering valuable insights to help users improve their sleep quality and overall well-being.

The Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach can analyze user data and provide personalized recommendations to help users achieve better rest and recovery. Both the AI Fitness Coach and the Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach are available through a subscription, with a three-month free trial offered to users who purchase the smartwatch.

As for technical specs, the watch comes with a 1.5-inch AMOLED display with a metallic frame. Weighing just 35g, it won’t feel heavy on your hand. The peak brightness is 1,500 nits, which isn't as high as offered in Apple watches, but still decent enough for regular use. Since the glass is anti-glare, even 1,500 nits of peak brightness would be sufficient in most cases.

The Amazfit Balance Smart Watch also comes with a range of hardware upgrades, including a dual-LED BioTracker 5.0 biometric optical sensor that can track mental and physical recovery status with high precision.

On top of that, it features body composition measurement capabilities, allowing it to track skeletal muscle mass, body fat percentage, muscle mass, water mass, bone mass, BMI, protein levels, and basal metabolism. That being said, it’s one of the best smartwatches for iPhone in 2023, especially under the $250 bracket.

Xiaomi Band 8
Cheap fitness tracker

A low-range Fitness tracker offering a wide range of features

$53 $70 Save $17

The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 presents a budget-friendly option with a 1.62-inch AMOLED screen and a smooth 60Hz refresh rate. The device impresses with a range of advanced sensors and a lightweight design.

Pros
  • 1.62-inch medium screen
  • Interactive user interface
  • Comes with a range of advanced sensors
Cons
  • Brightness maxes at 500 nits only
$53 at Amazon

The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 is the next-gen version of the Xiaomi Mi Band 7, and truly speaking, there's only a minor improvement. The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 features a larger and brighter 1.62-inch AMOLED screen with a resolution of 490 x 192 pixels, providing a more immersive and vibrant display compared to the Mi Band 7. The screen also supports an always-on display mode, allowing users to glance at their fitness and health data without having to wake up the device.

The Mi Band 8 has a sleek and modern design, with a pill-shaped tracker that measures 48 x 22.5 x 10.99mm, making it slightly longer and wider, but also thinner, than the Mi Band 7. The new model also features a 60Hz refresh rate and can reach a peak brightness of 600 nits, compared to the Mi Band 7's 500 nits. If you are someone who wants a fitness tracker to be used in peak sunlight, this one is not for you.

The Mi Band 8 is equipped with Bluetooth 5.1 and is compatible with iOS 12.0 and later. It also features 5ATM water resistance and weighs just 27g, making it one of the most lightweight fitness trackers of all time.

Xiaomi proudly claims a longer battery life of 16 days under typical usage and 6 days with AOD mode activated for Band 8. Additionally, the Band 8 introduces a new two-pin charger, ensuring a full charge for its 190 mAh battery in just one hour. However, it depends on what your real-time usage is.

The device comes with a range of sensors, including an optical heart rate sensor, a SpO2 sensor, and a sleep tracking sensor. The Mi Band 8 also comes with a range of smart features, including notifications, music control, and weather updates. Overall, I’d say go for it if you really don't have the budget to spare for a better one, since it’s just a basic one that fulfills the fundamental purpose of a fitness tracker.

Amazfit Bip 5
Extra large screen

An amazing option to bring up your fitness game

$75 $90 Save $15

The Amazfit Bip 5 is a vision for those seeking a striking smartwatch with its expansive 1.91-inch LCD display. With over 120 sports modes and a durable metal frame, it becomes a stylish and functional choice.

Pros
  • Large 1.91-inch display
  • 120+ sports modes
  • Metal frame provides durability
Cons
  • Large size makes it slightly heavy
$75 at Amazon

If you are someone who has a problem with eyesight, or just wants a fancy large screen for no particular reason, the Amazfit Bip 5 is your to-go choice. It features a large 1.91-inch LCD display which is not large - it's HUGE!

Coming to the design, the Bip 5 has a sleek and modern design, with a metal frame and a skin-friendly silicone strap. There are a total of 6 band options to choose from.

Some cool features about this smartwatch include 120+ sports modes that help users to track a wide range of activities and workouts. The Bip 5 can also automatically detect seven sports modes, making it easy to jump into action without needing to manually select a mode.

The device is equipped with a 300mAh battery which is claimed to run for up to 10 days on a single charge on regular usage(Realistically, just 6–7 days at max). But, here’s the great part: if you choose the power-saving mode, you can extend the life up to 26 days, as per manufacturer's claims.

As for the health aspects, it provides you with all the key metrics you are looking for in a fitness tracker, such as heart rate tracking, blood oxygen levels, and even menstrual cycles. Additionally, it can also be set to provide smart alerts for abnormal health readings, and can track sleep quality and stages in great detail. The Bip 5 also supports four satellite positioning systems to help users track their GPS movement.

For a smartwatch under $100, it really brings you value, especially with the large screen, long battery life, and multiple health detection metrics.

Choosing the right smartwatch

Picking the right smartwatch depends on factors like your lifestyle, desired features, and budget. Consider what you'll mainly use it for – fitness tracking, notifications, or even controlling music. A watch like the Apple Series 9 is truly an exceptional companion for all such needs, fulfilling every aspect while expanding on the concept of durability too.

Apple Watch Series 9
$390 $399 Save $9

Apple Watch Series 9 packs in a new, more powerful processor, a brighter display capable of up to 2,000 nits peak brightness, Apple's U2 chip that enables precision-finding of your iPhone, as well as better HomePod integration, and a whole slew of features and tech you'd expect.

$390 at Amazon

However, some people might want a more premium watch, for example, the Apple Watch Ultra 2. It matches the exact definition of “Luxury Redefined.” Being an all-in-one package loaded with all essential features as well as extras, it's specially designed for high-end users like professional athletes. However, not everybody has a high budget to afford an expensive gadget.

Switching to budget-friendly alternatives isn't really a bad idea, especially when the difference in quality is minimal. For example, the Amazfit Balance is a much cheaper option that offers quite similar features. But as you can see from this round-up of smartwatches, you aren't locked into using only wearables from Apple as an iPhone user. While those smartwatches offer integrations third-party options can't, brands like Garmin, Amazfit, and others can provide unique features Apple doesn't.

All in all, it really comes down to your requirements, as well as the budget you have in hand.

Smartwatches aren't just gadgets; they're a step towards a more efficient and well-balanced lifestyle. Offering an ingenious blend of technology and fashion, they have become a game-changer in enhancing our daily lives. From fitness tracking and heart rate monitoring to instant notifications and music control, you have everything on your wrist.

Nonetheless, so many options might be confusing, especially if you are an Apple user concerned about getting the right smartwatch for an iPhone. While Apple watches are a straightforward answer, there are other third-party smartwatches as well, offering you better durability and functionality.

  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Series 9
    Editor's choice
    $390 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Ultra 2
    Premium pick
    $774 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Midnight Apple Watch SE 2
    Best value
    $309 at Walmart
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Venu 3
    Best for runners
    $450 at Amazon
  • Source: Fitbit
    Fitbit Charge 5
    Best fitness tracker
    $144 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit Balance
    AI powered fitness coach
    $200 at Amazon
  • Source: Xiaomi
    Xiaomi Band 8
    Cheap fitness tracker
    $53 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit Bip 5
    Extra large screen
    $75 at Amazon

Our top picks for best smartwatches for the iPhone

Apple Watch Series 9
Editor's choice

The ultimate watch for an outstanding performance and value

$390 $399 Save $9

The Apple Watch Series 9 stands out with its blazing S9 chip, boasting a 2x brighter display at 2000 nits. With advanced features like ECG checks and all-day battery life, it's the perfect blend of performance and practicality for iPhone users seeking a mid-range smartwatch.

Pros
  • New Apple S9 Chip
  • Brighter screen at 2,000 nits
  • Wide variety of case colors
Cons
  • Battery life could be improved further
$390 at Amazon

Apple Watch Series 9 is the latest addition to the list of Apple Watches, released in September 2023. What’s new? Well, for starters, it has a 2x brighter display than the Series 8, with a maximum brightness of up to 2000 nits, making it easier to read in full sun and better in low-light situations. The display size is 1.69 inches, which is neither too big nor too small. Plus, It also features a new Double Tap gesture, which allows users to launch apps and control the watch with a simple tap of their thumb and forefinger.

Compared to the Apple Watch Series 8, the Series 9 has a faster S9 chip. The S9 chip is the most powerful watch chip yet, with a dual-core CPU that has 5.6 billion transistors and a four-core Neural Engine that can process machine learning tasks up to twice as fast as the S8 chip. The Series 9 also has expanded Siri processing and the option of a fun pink case and band.

The Series 9 has a 64GB RAM capacity. Screen size variations include 45mm and 41mm, but with a marginally thin price difference, my recommendation would be the 45mm version. Best part? You get to choose your case type. Apple is offering users to select from stainless steel or aluminum cases. But, obviously, stainless steel is much more expensive, as compared to aluminum.

With several advanced features, such as ECG check, time spent in deep sleep, blood oxygen level monitoring, and several others, it is by-far one of the best smartwatches for iPhone in 2023.

With up to 18 hours of battery life (for GPS+Cellular variant), you’ll be able to use it for the entire day easily. In a nutshell, it really is the ideal choice for someone who cannot put in a big chunk of money for Apple Watch Ultra or Ultra 2, but still wants a decent Apple smartwatch.

Apple Watch Ultra 2
Premium pick

An excellent choice for managing fitness with luxury

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 shines with a dazzling 3000-nit display and a robust titanium case, offering both style and durability. It's the top-tier choice for those seeking a brilliant smartwatch experience.

Pros
  • Maximum brightness of 3000 nits
  • Titanium case gives it maximum protection
  • Double tap gesture
Cons
  • Priciest smartwatch for iPhone
$774 at Amazon

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 is a premium smartwatch offering from Apple, featuring a range of advanced capabilities. The Ultra 2 has a brighter display, with a maximum brightness of up to 3000 nits, compared to the original Ultra's 2000-nit display. It also features a 49mm corrosion-resistant titanium case which gives it points for durability. Also, multiple colors and sizes enable you precise customization.

GPS and Cellular connectivity are both provided, and it offers 40m water-resistance too, which makes it ideal for water sports enthusiasts. As for health management, this watch is packed with loads of metrics including ECG, blood sugar, and heart health monitor.

When compared to the previous Apple Watch Ultra, the Ultra 2 offers several improvements, including the new S9 SiP with a 64-bit dual-core processor, a customizable Action button, and Double Tap gesture as well. Another new thing we notice is Precision Finding for iPhone 15 models, which is not available in the original Ultra.

As far as the battery is concerned, Apple claims a 36-hour battery life on regular use, and 72-hour battery life in low-power mode. Realistically speaking, I would say it would be more like 30 hours and 60 hours, respectively. Nonetheless, it really depends on your usage. But one thing is for sure, the battery timing is ample to keep you running the entire day even with ample use, and even good enough when traveling long distances.

One thing to really consider is the high weight of 61.4g, which may be of inconvenience to a few users. Specifically, I would recommend this watch to someone looking for a really bright display, since no other Apple Watch comes at a peak brightness of 3000 nits. Plus, the battery timing is really great. The other features are also available in Apple Watch Series 9, such as the S9 Chip.

Midnight Apple Watch SE 2
Best value

A low-priced watch for Apple fans

The Apple Watch SE 2 offers GPS + Cellular models and sleek aluminum finishes. It comes loaded with features including heart rate monitoring, water resistance, and fall detection. The Retina OLED display, powerful chip, and sustainable design make it a versatile and eco-conscious wearable for someone wanting an Apple product on a budget.

Pros
  • Faster charging support (0-80% in 45 minutes)
  • Water-resistant up to 50m
  • 4 times cheaper than Apple Watch Ultra
Cons
  • Does not have Always-on Display
$309 at Walmart

There’s always this sense of trust when it comes to official products, and for that reason, the top 2 of our picks are official Apple products. While it may not be the best, it surely is one of the finest budget picks since it offers 70% of the features offered on Apple Watch Ultra, and is quite identical to Apple Watch Series 8.

The second-generation Apple Watch SE maintains the 44mm and 40mm case options and retains the display size of its predecessors. Offering a resolution of 368x448 pixels, you won’t feel like making a compromise on the display. The Retina LTPO OLED display itself is indeed a cool aspect for many users.

However, it offers half the brightness of the Apple Watch Ultra at 1000 nits. It isn’t really bad unless you are really looking forward to using your watch in extreme lighting conditions.

The real improvement lies within the processor as Apple Watch SE 2 comes with S8 SiP with a 64-bit dual-core processor. This is followed by the W3 Apple Wireless Chip as well. A few tests have shown a 20% improvement in the performance and speed of the Apple Watch SE 2 compared to its predecessors.

The 36-hour battery life is a little less compared to other watches. However, considering all other aspects, including fitness tracking and emergency crash detection, the Apple Watch SE 2 is a reasonable bargain for someone wanting an Apple product on a budget.

Garmin Venu 3
Best for runners

Sophistication redefined with an impressive battery timing

At half the price of Apple Ultra, you can stay ahead with Garmin's cutting-edge wearable technology. With an extensive focus on health aspects and an amazing battery backup of 11 days, it becomes a dynamic companion for your active lifestyle.

Pros
  • 11-day mega battery backup
  • Provides a fuller picture of one's health
  • 1 hour fast charging support
Cons
  • No map support
$450 at Amazon

The Garmin Venu 3 is the latest addition to Garmin's Venu series of GPS smartwatches and a pretty smart choice for runners. Why? Mainly because the Venu 3 introduces new health and fitness features, including Sleep Coaching, Recovery Time, and Wheelchair Mode, which are not available on the Venu 2.

The Venu 3 comes with a built-in speaker and microphone, allowing users to answer calls and interact with voice assistants directly from the watch. The Venu 3 has a diameter of 45.4 mm and a thickness of 12.2 mm. On top of that, the Venu 3 features a high display resolution of 454 x 454 pixels, providing sharper and more detailed images and text compared to the Venu 2, which had 416 x 416 pixels.

You have the option to choose from 45mm and 41mm. The Venu 3 features a vibrant and sharp 1.4-inch AMOLED touchscreen display which provides a brilliant interface for various activities, from checking notifications to tracking workouts. With up to 14 days of battery life in smartwatch mode, the Venu 3 offers impressive longevity, reducing the need for frequent recharging. However, real-time results will vary.

In addition to that, Venu 3 provides detailed health and wellness insights, including features like Body Battery and advanced sleep monitoring. Body Battery informs users about their energy levels, helping them determine the optimal time for training, while advanced sleep monitoring offers valuable data to improve sleep quality and overall well-being.

Fitbit Charge 5
Best fitness tracker

A lightweight and light-featured watch recommended for beginners

$144 $150 Save $6

For a low price tag, this is definitely one of the best fitness trackers for someone not much into extra features. The Fitbit Charge 5 may not have all the features you’ve heard about, but it does fulfill the basic job: Health and Fitness tracking.

Pros
  • Does not feel too heavy
  • ECG Application Supported
  • 7 days battery life
Cons
  • No physical button on the back
  • Smaller screen than other known watches
  • Does not support music playback
$144 at Amazon

The Fitbit Charge 5 is one of the best fitness trackers on the market and is an ideal choice for someone looking to spend minimally on a smartwatch. However, that doesn’t mean you are compromising on any major element. A 7-day battery backup followed by several fitness tracking parameters makes it quite simple for any user to stay engaged in their wellness journey.

The fact that this watch has ECG supported is kind of unbelievable, noticing the fairly lower price point. It also tracks the trends in your wellness and health aspects by monitoring heart rate variation, breathing rate, pulse rate, and numerous other indicators.

You’ll also notice extra applications offered on this watch to enhance the user experience. Smart notifications, Find My Phone, Silent Alarms, and Smart Wakeup are just a few to name.

However, Charge 5 makes its way down the list because users aren't really impressed by the 14.7mm x 21.9mm screen size. Besides, the user interface itself isn’t neat. Only one of nine metrics is visible at one time, and to access the next metric, a tap is required. Imagine: While you are running, the small screen size makes the text illegible. It just eliminates the point of convenience.

Another downside to this watch is it does not have support for Spotify or music streaming, unlike other popular smartwatches in the market. Overall, it isn't really bad as a smartwatch, given the low price point. It does fulfill the basic purpose of health and fitness tracking.

Amazfit Balance
AI powered fitness coach

Built for the rough and tough ones

$200 $230 Save $30

The Amazfit Balance Smartwatch takes fitness to the next level with its AI-powered fitness coach and an exceptional 1.5-inch AMOLED display, all packed into a lightweight design for ultimate convenience.

Pros
  • Lots of helpful AI features
  • 1.5-inch stunning AMOLED display
  • Lightweight and convenient
Cons
  • No blood pressure monitoring
$200 at Amazon

It's 2023, and we are already seeing a massive rise of AI in modern gadgets and applications, even in smartwatches. The Amazfit Balance Smartwatch is a cutting-edge fitness and health tracker that comes with an AI Fitness Coach. It leverages large language models (LLMs) to provide personalized guidance and support to users. The AI Fitness Coach utilizes user data to generate detailed reports and can offer clear and straightforward answers to user queries.

In addition to the AI Fitness Coach, the Amazfit Balance Smart Watch also features an AI-powered chatbot known as the Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach. This chatbot is designed to provide guidance on sleep and wellness, offering valuable insights to help users improve their sleep quality and overall well-being.

The Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach can analyze user data and provide personalized recommendations to help users achieve better rest and recovery. Both the AI Fitness Coach and the Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach are available through a subscription, with a three-month free trial offered to users who purchase the smartwatch.

As for technical specs, the watch comes with a 1.5-inch AMOLED display with a metallic frame. Weighing just 35g, it won’t feel heavy on your hand. The peak brightness is 1,500 nits, which isn't as high as offered in Apple watches, but still decent enough for regular use. Since the glass is anti-glare, even 1,500 nits of peak brightness would be sufficient in most cases.

The Amazfit Balance Smart Watch also comes with a range of hardware upgrades, including a dual-LED BioTracker 5.0 biometric optical sensor that can track mental and physical recovery status with high precision.

On top of that, it features body composition measurement capabilities, allowing it to track skeletal muscle mass, body fat percentage, muscle mass, water mass, bone mass, BMI, protein levels, and basal metabolism. That being said, it’s one of the best smartwatches for iPhone in 2023, especially under the $250 bracket.

Xiaomi Band 8
Cheap fitness tracker

A low-range Fitness tracker offering a wide range of features

$53 $70 Save $17

The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 presents a budget-friendly option with a 1.62-inch AMOLED screen and a smooth 60Hz refresh rate. The device impresses with a range of advanced sensors and a lightweight design.

Pros
  • 1.62-inch medium screen
  • Interactive user interface
  • Comes with a range of advanced sensors
Cons
  • Brightness maxes at 500 nits only
$53 at Amazon

The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 is the next-gen version of the Xiaomi Mi Band 7, and truly speaking, there's only a minor improvement. The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 features a larger and brighter 1.62-inch AMOLED screen with a resolution of 490 x 192 pixels, providing a more immersive and vibrant display compared to the Mi Band 7. The screen also supports an always-on display mode, allowing users to glance at their fitness and health data without having to wake up the device.

The Mi Band 8 has a sleek and modern design, with a pill-shaped tracker that measures 48 x 22.5 x 10.99mm, making it slightly longer and wider, but also thinner, than the Mi Band 7. The new model also features a 60Hz refresh rate and can reach a peak brightness of 600 nits, compared to the Mi Band 7's 500 nits. If you are someone who wants a fitness tracker to be used in peak sunlight, this one is not for you.

The Mi Band 8 is equipped with Bluetooth 5.1 and is compatible with iOS 12.0 and later. It also features 5ATM water resistance and weighs just 27g, making it one of the most lightweight fitness trackers of all time.

Xiaomi proudly claims a longer battery life of 16 days under typical usage and 6 days with AOD mode activated for Band 8. Additionally, the Band 8 introduces a new two-pin charger, ensuring a full charge for its 190 mAh battery in just one hour. However, it depends on what your real-time usage is.

The device comes with a range of sensors, including an optical heart rate sensor, a SpO2 sensor, and a sleep tracking sensor. The Mi Band 8 also comes with a range of smart features, including notifications, music control, and weather updates. Overall, I’d say go for it if you really don't have the budget to spare for a better one, since it’s just a basic one that fulfills the fundamental purpose of a fitness tracker.

Amazfit Bip 5
Extra large screen

An amazing option to bring up your fitness game

$75 $90 Save $15

The Amazfit Bip 5 is a vision for those seeking a striking smartwatch with its expansive 1.91-inch LCD display. With over 120 sports modes and a durable metal frame, it becomes a stylish and functional choice.

Pros
  • Large 1.91-inch display
  • 120+ sports modes
  • Metal frame provides durability
Cons
  • Large size makes it slightly heavy
$75 at Amazon

If you are someone who has a problem with eyesight, or just wants a fancy large screen for no particular reason, the Amazfit Bip 5 is your to-go choice. It features a large 1.91-inch LCD display which is not large - it's HUGE!

Coming to the design, the Bip 5 has a sleek and modern design, with a metal frame and a skin-friendly silicone strap. There are a total of 6 band options to choose from.

Some cool features about this smartwatch include 120+ sports modes that help users to track a wide range of activities and workouts. The Bip 5 can also automatically detect seven sports modes, making it easy to jump into action without needing to manually select a mode.

The device is equipped with a 300mAh battery which is claimed to run for up to 10 days on a single charge on regular usage(Realistically, just 6–7 days at max). But, here’s the great part: if you choose the power-saving mode, you can extend the life up to 26 days, as per manufacturer's claims.

As for the health aspects, it provides you with all the key metrics you are looking for in a fitness tracker, such as heart rate tracking, blood oxygen levels, and even menstrual cycles. Additionally, it can also be set to provide smart alerts for abnormal health readings, and can track sleep quality and stages in great detail. The Bip 5 also supports four satellite positioning systems to help users track their GPS movement.

For a smartwatch under $100, it really brings you value, especially with the large screen, long battery life, and multiple health detection metrics.

Choosing the right smartwatch

Picking the right smartwatch depends on factors like your lifestyle, desired features, and budget. Consider what you'll mainly use it for – fitness tracking, notifications, or even controlling music. A watch like the Apple Series 9 is truly an exceptional companion for all such needs, fulfilling every aspect while expanding on the concept of durability too.

Apple Watch Series 9
$390 $399 Save $9

Apple Watch Series 9 packs in a new, more powerful processor, a brighter display capable of up to 2,000 nits peak brightness, Apple's U2 chip that enables precision-finding of your iPhone, as well as better HomePod integration, and a whole slew of features and tech you'd expect.

$390 at Amazon

However, some people might want a more premium watch, for example, the Apple Watch Ultra 2. It matches the exact definition of “Luxury Redefined.” Being an all-in-one package loaded with all essential features as well as extras, it's specially designed for high-end users like professional athletes. However, not everybody has a high budget to afford an expensive gadget.

Switching to budget-friendly alternatives isn't really a bad idea, especially when the difference in quality is minimal. For example, the Amazfit Balance is a much cheaper option that offers quite similar features. But as you can see from this round-up of smartwatches, you aren't locked into using only wearables from Apple as an iPhone user. While those smartwatches offer integrations third-party options can't, brands like Garmin, Amazfit, and others can provide unique features Apple doesn't.

All in all, it really comes down to your requirements, as well as the budget you have in hand.

The AI Fitness Coach utilizes user data to generate detailed reports and can offer clear and straightforward answers to user queries.

In addition to the AI Fitness Coach, the Amazfit Balance Smart Watch also features an AI-powered chatbot known as the Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach. This chatbot is designed to provide guidance on sleep and wellness, offering valuable insights to help users improve their sleep quality and overall well-being.

The Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach can analyze user data and provide personalized recommendations to help users achieve better rest and recovery. Both the AI Fitness Coach and the Zepp Aura AI Wellness Coach are available through a subscription, with a three-month free trial offered to users who purchase the smartwatch.

As for technical specs, the watch comes with a 1.5-inch AMOLED display with a metallic frame. Weighing just 35g, it won’t feel heavy on your hand. The peak brightness is 1,500 nits, which isn't as high as offered in Apple watches, but still decent enough for regular use. Since the glass is anti-glare, even 1,500 nits of peak brightness would be sufficient in most cases.

The Amazfit Balance Smart Watch also comes with a range of hardware upgrades, including a dual-LED BioTracker 5.0 biometric optical sensor that can track mental and physical recovery status with high precision.

On top of that, it features body composition measurement capabilities, allowing it to track skeletal muscle mass, body fat percentage, muscle mass, water mass, bone mass, BMI, protein levels, and basal metabolism. That being said, it’s one of the best smartwatches for iPhone in 2023, especially under the $250 bracket.

Xiaomi Band 8
Cheap fitness tracker

A low-range Fitness tracker offering a wide range of features

$53 $70 Save $17

The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 presents a budget-friendly option with a 1.62-inch AMOLED screen and a smooth 60Hz refresh rate. The device impresses with a range of advanced sensors and a lightweight design.

Pros
  • 1.62-inch medium screen
  • Interactive user interface
  • Comes with a range of advanced sensors
Cons
  • Brightness maxes at 500 nits only
$53 at Amazon

The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 is the next-gen version of the Xiaomi Mi Band 7, and truly speaking, there's only a minor improvement. The Xiaomi Mi Band 8 features a larger and brighter 1.62-inch AMOLED screen with a resolution of 490 x 192 pixels, providing a more immersive and vibrant display compared to the Mi Band 7. The screen also supports an always-on display mode, allowing users to glance at their fitness and health data without having to wake up the device.

The Mi Band 8 has a sleek and modern design, with a pill-shaped tracker that measures 48 x 22.5 x 10.99mm, making it slightly longer and wider, but also thinner, than the Mi Band 7. The new model also features a 60Hz refresh rate and can reach a peak brightness of 600 nits, compared to the Mi Band 7's 500 nits. If you are someone who wants a fitness tracker to be used in peak sunlight, this one is not for you.

The Mi Band 8 is equipped with Bluetooth 5.1 and is compatible with iOS 12.0 and later. It also features 5ATM water resistance and weighs just 27g, making it one of the most lightweight fitness trackers of all time.

Xiaomi proudly claims a longer battery life of 16 days under typical usage and 6 days with AOD mode activated for Band 8. Additionally, the Band 8 introduces a new two-pin charger, ensuring a full charge for its 190 mAh battery in just one hour. However, it depends on what your real-time usage is.

The device comes with a range of sensors, including an optical heart rate sensor, a SpO2 sensor, and a sleep tracking sensor. The Mi Band 8 also comes with a range of smart features, including notifications, music control, and weather updates. Overall, I’d say go for it if you really don't have the budget to spare for a better one, since it’s just a basic one that fulfills the fundamental purpose of a fitness tracker.

Amazfit Bip 5
Extra large screen

An amazing option to bring up your fitness game

$75 $90 Save $15

The Amazfit Bip 5 is a vision for those seeking a striking smartwatch with its expansive 1.91-inch LCD display. With over 120 sports modes and a durable metal frame, it becomes a stylish and functional choice.

Pros
  • Large 1.91-inch display
  • 120+ sports modes
  • Metal frame provides durability
Cons
  • Large size makes it slightly heavy
$75 at Amazon

If you are someone who has a problem with eyesight, or just wants a fancy large screen for no particular reason, the Amazfit Bip 5 is your to-go choice. It features a large 1.91-inch LCD display which is not large - it's HUGE!

Coming to the design, the Bip 5 has a sleek and modern design, with a metal frame and a skin-friendly silicone strap. There are a total of 6 band options to choose from.

Some cool features about this smartwatch include 120+ sports modes that help users to track a wide range of activities and workouts. The Bip 5 can also automatically detect seven sports modes, making it easy to jump into action without needing to manually select a mode.

The device is equipped with a 300mAh battery which is claimed to run for up to 10 days on a single charge on regular usage(Realistically, just 6–7 days at max). But, here’s the great part: if you choose the power-saving mode, you can extend the life up to 26 days, as per manufacturer's claims.

As for the health aspects, it provides you with all the key metrics you are looking for in a fitness tracker, such as heart rate tracking, blood oxygen levels, and even menstrual cycles. Additionally, it can also be set to provide smart alerts for abnormal health readings, and can track sleep quality and stages in great detail. The Bip 5 also supports four satellite positioning systems to help users track their GPS movement.

For a smartwatch under $100, it really brings you value, especially with the large screen, long battery life, and multiple health detection metrics.

Choosing the right smartwatch

Picking the right smartwatch depends on factors like your lifestyle, desired features, and budget. Consider what you'll mainly use it for – fitness tracking, notifications, or even controlling music. A watch like the Apple Series 9 is truly an exceptional companion for all such needs, fulfilling every aspect while expanding on the concept of durability too.

Apple Watch Series 9
$390 $399 Save $9

Apple Watch Series 9 packs in a new, more powerful processor, a brighter display capable of up to 2,000 nits peak brightness, Apple's U2 chip that enables precision-finding of your iPhone, as well as better HomePod integration, and a whole slew of features and tech you'd expect.

$390 at Amazon

However, some people might want a more premium watch, for example, the Apple Watch Ultra 2. It matches the exact definition of “Luxury Redefined.” Being an all-in-one package loaded with all essential features as well as extras, it's specially designed for high-end users like professional athletes. However, not everybody has a high budget to afford an expensive gadget.

Switching to budget-friendly alternatives isn't really a bad idea, especially when the difference in quality is minimal. For example, the Amazfit Balance is a much cheaper option that offers quite similar features. But as you can see from this round-up of smartwatches, you aren't locked into using only wearables from Apple as an iPhone user. While those smartwatches offer integrations third-party options can't, brands like Garmin, Amazfit, and others can provide unique features Apple doesn't.

All in all, it really comes down to your requirements, as well as the budget you have in hand.

item.title: How to insert a SIM card in your iPhone
item.itunes:title: How to insert a SIM card in your iPhone
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
When you buy an iPhone, you typically expect it to make and receive calls. However, for it to actually be able to connect to your carrier's network and utilize its services, you may need to insert a physical SIM card. The process can be ambiguous for some people, so we've detailed the steps you have to follow below.

How to insert a SIM card in your iPhone

To get started, you will need your iPhone, SIM card, and the SIM ejection tool included in your iPhone's box. If you can't find the SIM ejection tool, then a paperclip or pin would work as an alternative.

  1. Turn off your iPhone if it's already on. Hold the Side button on the left and the Volume Up button on the right at the same time until you get the screen shown below.
  2. Slide from left to right on the Power button that appears on the screen.
  3. Remove your iPhone's case, if you have one, and locate the SIM slot. Depending on its model, you will find a tiny hole on either the right or left side of your iPhone.
  4. Insert the SIM ejection tool or pin right into the SIM slot hole.
  5. Once the SIM tray pops out, remove the pin and gently slide it out with your fingers.
  6. Place the SIM card on the tray with the bronze side facing up. You can only place it in one position because the SIM card and tray have a missing corner (rather than being a perfect rectangle).
  7. Insert the SIM tray into the iPhone with the bronze side still facing up.
  8. Slide it completely into the iPhone.
  9. Put the case back on, and hold on the Side button for a few seconds until the Apple logo shows up.
  10. That's it! Your phone will be ready to use within a few seconds of seeing the logo, and you should be able to use your cellular plan.

The bottom line

For those who don't use the eSIM technology, SIM card insertion is quite essential. After all, without a SIM, a user won't be able to call and text their contacts or rely on mobile data. That's why inserting a SIM card is usually the first step people take after buying a new phone.

It's worth noting that iPhone 14 and newer models (including the latest iPhone 15 Pro Max) sold in the U.S. don't have a physical SIM tray. So, if you can't find the SIM slot, you will have to depend on an eSIM to use carrier services, such as making/receiving calls, exchanging SMS texts, and taking advantage of 4G/5G data.
