item.title: Best HP computer deals in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best HP computer deals in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
HP is one of the biggest PC builders around, with a massive catalog of products including everything from high-end laptop and desktop PCs to printers and monitors. We're focusing on HP's best computer deals for this curated roundup, which includes high-end brands like Spectre, Dragonfly, EliteBook, and Omen as well as more budget-minded brands like Envy, Pavilion, and Victus.

HP has a device for pretty much everyone, and with deals cycling in and out at HP's official website and at third-party retailers, you shouldn't have to wait long to find the perfect computer at a discounted price. Black Friday and Cyber Monday brought some great deals on HP laptops at Best Buy and Amazon, not to mention the official HP website. It looks like some of those deals are carrying through to help with late holiday shopping. Here are the best HP laptop deals going to help you shop the right way.

Best HP laptop deals in December 2023

HP has a wide range of laptops, and there's bound to be something that catches your eye in this roundup of great HP laptop deals.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
$1350 $1750 Save $400

Best Buy currently has a monster deal on the 13.5.-inch Spectre x360 convertible. For $1,350 you get a 13th Gen Intel Core i7-1355U CPU, 16GB of LPDDR4x RAM, 1TB M.2 PCIe SSD, and 13.5-inch OLED touch display with 3000x2000 resolution. It's an outstanding laptop discounted by $400.

$1350 at Best Buy
HP Envy 17 (2023)
$850 $1250 Save $400

You can pick up this HP Envy 17 for $400 off the regular price, bringing it down to $850. The discounted model has a 13th Gen Intel Core i7-13700H CPU, 16GB of dual-channel DDR4 RAM, 512GB M.2 PCIe NVMe SSD, and a 17.3-inch FHD display with 300 nits brightness and 100% sRGB color. It's one of the best 17-inch laptops out there today.

$850 at HP
HP Pavilion 15
$550 $1010 Save $460

The latest 15.6-inch HP Pavilion with 13th Gen Intel Core i7-1355U CPU, 16GB of DDR4 RAM, 256GB M.2 PCIe SSD, and FHD display with anti-glare finish is down to just $550 from the usual $1,010 price. You can configure as you'd like with more powerful hardware and still keep the flat $460 discount.

$550 at HP
HP Pavilion Aero 13 (2023)
$480 $930 Save $450

The Pavilion Aero is a super light and compact 13-inch laptop, always attractive thanks to its bargain pricing. HP currently has it discounted by a flat $450, bringing the introductory price down to $480 for a model with Ryzen 5 7535U CPU, FHD+ display, 8GB of RAM, and 256GB SSD.

$480 at HP
HP EliteBook 845 G9
$999 $2507 Save $1508

This high-end business laptop from HP has an AMD Ryzen 5 Pro 6650U CPU, 16GB of RAM, 512GB SSD, AMD Radeon 660M graphics, and a 14-inch display with FHD+ resolution and 100% sRGB color. It's down to just $999 from the usual $2,507 price.

$999 at HP
HP Omen 16 (2023)
$1070 $1570 Save $500

HP's Omen 16 (2023) with AMD hardware has been discounted by a flat $500 at HP's website. Prices start at $1,070 for a unit with AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS CPU, 16GB of LPDDR5 RAM, 512GB M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD, Nvidia RTX 4060 Laptop GPU, and a 16-inch FHD display at 165Hz. Spec up as you'd like and still save the $500.

$1070 at HP
HP ZBook Power G10
$2519 $6014 Save $3495

This monster mobile worktstation features an Intel Core i7-13800H CPU, 64GB of DDR5 RAM, 2TB of M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD storage, Nvidia A2000 GPU, and a 15.6-inch QHD display. Regularly pried at $6,014, it's been discounted by $3,495.

$2519 at HP

Best HP desktop deals for December 2023

HP Omen 40L
$1030 $1500 Save $470

This pre-built gaming rig has a 13th Gen Intel Core i5-13400 CPU, Nvidia RTX 4060 GPU, 16GB of Kingston Fury DDR5-5200MHz RAM, and a 512GB WD Black M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD. You can pick it up for $1,030 instead of $1,500, but the same $470 discount will apply as you configure as needed.

$1030 at HP
HP Victus 15L
$430 $780 Save $350

Victus is HP's budget gaming brand, and it lives up to the categorization with this $430 desktop deal. It has an AMD Ryzen 5 5600G CPU, AMD Radeon RX 6400 GPU, 8GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 256GB M.2 PCIe SSD. It's $350 off and should make a great starter PC for those just getting into gaming.

$430 at HP
HP Envy Move
$749 $899 Save $150

The Envy Move is a brand new all-in-one PC that focuses on mobility. It's sort of like a laptop that grew legs. A model with Core i3-1315U CPU, 8GB of RAM, 256GB SSD, and 23.8-inch QHD display is already down to $749 from the usual $899 price.

$749 at HP
HP 27" Touch-Screen All-In-One
$650 $850 Save $200

HP's 27-inch all-in-one PC with AMD Ryzen 5 5500U CPU, 8GB of RAM, 256GB M.2 PCIe SSD, and 27-inch FHD display is now down to just $650 from the usual $850 price. It doesn't have the latest AMD chips, but it's still a great affordable option to have at home or at the office. An Intel model is also on sale at Best Buy for $700.

$650 at HP $700 at Best Buy
HP ENVY 34 All-in-One (2022)
$1530 $2000 Save $470

HP's 34-inch All-in-One (AiO) PC features a gorgeous 5K display with 21:9 aspect ratio, 16MP detachable webcam, 12th Gen Intel Core CPUs, Nvidia discrete GPUs, and plenty of memory and storage. You can configure the model as you'd like at HP's site and still get the flat $470 discount. Prices start at about $1,530 for one of the best all-in-one PCs around.

$1530 at HP
HP Pavilion 27 AiO
$700 $950 Save $250

The 27-inch Pavilion AiO is a more affordable option made better by a $250 discount. The introductory model has an Intel Core i3-13100T CPU, Nvidia MX450 discrete GPU, 8GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 256GB SSD. You can upgrade to a Core i7 CPU, RTX 3050 GPU, 32GB of RAM, and 3TB of storage all while keeping the same $250 discount.

$700 at HP

FAQ

Q: When do the best HP computer deals occur?

HP is almost always hosting some sort of sale for its laptop and desktop computers, and it's always a good idea to wait for a deal to pick up a new device. Major sales events like Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and Prime Day are always full of opportunities to save, but it's never a bad idea to check retailers throughout the year for any temporary deals on a computer of your choice. Major holidays and social events (like back-to-school season) are a great time to shop as well, and we're making sure that the deals in this curated list stay current.

Q: Is it best to shop at HP's website?

HP's website is a great place to shop for computers. There are almost always some great laptop deals available, and you can often take a deal item, configure it as you'd like, and still get the same flat discount. It's a great way to get exactly what you want for less money. HP, however, doesn't always have the lowest prices. Best Buy, Amazon, B&H, and Walmart all host a lot of HP products, and you can often find cheaper prices at third-party retailers. These retailers won't usually have the customizable option, and you'll have to be OK with a pre-configured model.

Q: How can you tell that you're getting a good deal?

While it's always easy to compare Amazon's pricing with CamelCamelCamel — which displays pricing history for the product — shopping at other retailers isn't as straightforward. HP will often add a badge to products advertising a new low price at its official site, but it's not a sure bet. Deal guides like these can help you find the right product at the right price without having to do all the legwork yourself. You might not getting the absolute lowest product price in history, but you can be sure you're getting a killer deal on a laptop that's worth your money.

$700 at HP

FAQ

Q: When do the best HP computer deals occur?

HP is almost always hosting some sort of sale for its laptop and desktop computers, and it's always a good idea to wait for a deal to pick up a new device. Major sales events like Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and Prime Day are always full of opportunities to save, but it's never a bad idea to check retailers throughout the year for any temporary deals on a computer of your choice. Major holidays and social events (like back-to-school season) are a great time to shop as well, and we're making sure that the deals in this curated list stay current.

Q: Is it best to shop at HP's website?

HP's website is a great place to shop for computers. There are almost always some great laptop deals available, and you can often take a deal item, configure it as you'd like, and still get the same flat discount. It's a great way to get exactly what you want for less money. HP, however, doesn't always have the lowest prices. Best Buy, Amazon, B&H, and Walmart all host a lot of HP products, and you can often find cheaper prices at third-party retailers. These retailers won't usually have the customizable option, and you'll have to be OK with a pre-configured model.

Q: How can you tell that you're getting a good deal?

While it's always easy to compare Amazon's pricing with CamelCamelCamel — which displays pricing history for the product — shopping at other retailers isn't as straightforward. HP will often add a badge to products advertising a new low price at its official site, but it's not a sure bet. Deal guides like these can help you find the right product at the right price without having to do all the legwork yourself. You might not getting the absolute lowest product price in history, but you can be sure you're getting a killer deal on a laptop that's worth your money.

item.title: Best Ayaneo Air Plus chargers
item.itunes:title: Best Ayaneo Air Plus chargers
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Ayaneo Air Plus is a PC gaming handheld console and a mighty competitor of the Steam Deck. The charger that you get right out of the box is great, but it's always worth having multiple chargers, especially in the case of a portable PC gaming console. The Ayaneo Air Plus charges over a USB-C port; according to the specifications, it requires a 60W charger. This means any charger you pick up for the Ayaneo Air Plus will also be capable of charging a laptop, tablet, phone, or any other device that supports charging over USB-C. To help you out, we've curated a list of great chargers for the Ayaneo Air Plus.

  • Nekteck 60W USB-C Charger
    Editor's choice
    $24 at Amazon
  • Ugreen 100W 4-Port GaN Wall Charger

    $70 at Amazon

  • Satechi 200W USB-C 6 Port GaN Charger
    Best charging dock

    Compact charger
    $60 at Amazon
  • Baseus 65W USB-C car charger
    Best car charger

  • Ayaneo Air Plus
    $979 at AYANEO

item.title: How to remove the Xbox app in Windows 11
item.itunes:title: How to remove the Xbox app in Windows 11
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Windows 11 is great for gaming, especially since you have access to a lot of great titles through Xbox Game Pass in the Xbox app. However, the app can be a bit buggy at times. I've had my fair share of problems — random crashes, extremely slow download speeds, and problems updating certain games. Usually, a quick reinstallation fixes the issue. If you're running into the same problems, or aren't a gamer and have no use for the Xbox app, here's how you can remove it in Windows 11.

How to remove the Xbox app in Windows 11 using the Settings app

Uninstalling the Xbox app in Windows 11 is the same as uninstalling any app in Windows 10 and 11. If you're not familiar with the process, follow the steps below to completely remove the Xbox app from your system.

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Click Apps from the left sidebar.
  3. In the Apps menu, select click Installed apps.
  4. Type "Xbox" into the search bar, right-click on the three dots next to the app, and select Uninstall from the drop-down menu.
  5. Windows will ask you for a confirmation, so click on Uninstall once again to remove the app.

Now, if you search for the Xbox app in the Start menu, you'll find that it has been successfully removed from your system. You can quickly reinstall the app from the Microsoft Store whenever you want.

How to remove the Xbox app in Windows 11 using PowerShell

The traditional method of uninstalling apps works just fine for the Xbox app, but that's not always the case. Sometimes, Windows will automatically download and reinstall the app in the background without asking you. If you want to permanently remove the Xbox app for good, you can use a few commands in PowerShell to get the job done.

  1. From the Start menu, search for PowerShell by typing it into the search bar.
  2. Click Run as Administrator, and click Yes on the confirmation dialog box that pops up afterward.
  3. Use any of these three commands to successfully remove the Xbox app from your system:
get-appxpackage *xboxapp* | remove-appxpackage

This command uninstalls the Xbox app for the current user.

get-appxpackage -allusers *xboxapp* | Remove-AppxPackage

This command uninstalls the Xbox app for all user accounts on your system.

get-appxprovisionedpackage –online | where-object {$_.packagename –like “*xboxapp*”} | remove-appxprovisionedpackage –onlinedsda

This command uninstalls the Xbox app for all current user accounts and also prevents it from being installed for user accounts created in the future.

All of these commands are safe and will only affect the Xbox app. You don't need to worry about messing up your system.

Getting rid of the Xbox app in Windows 11

Microsoft's native Windows 11 apps and features are useful, but they can be a bit buggy and intrusive at times. The Xbox app is great when it works, but terribly frustrating when it doesn't. When the app eventually starts giving you a headache, just follow the methods above to quickly uninstall it. We recommend the PowerShell method if you want to get rid of the app for good. While you're at it, you might as well disable Xbox Game Bar if you don't get much use out of it.
